revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[平永]]1197 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 44.2 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 33 |経度秒 = 30.0 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじん…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[平永]]1197 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 44.2 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 33 |経度秒 = 30.0 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[加須市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）に創建された。[[館林藩]]初代[[藩主]][[榊原康政]]の家臣に「松崎勘兵衛」という人物がおり、学問・人徳に優れていた。康政は勘兵衛を評価し、[[後陽成天皇]]筆の[[菅原道真]]像の[[掛軸]]を与えた。その後、道真と勘兵衛を慕う村人によって神社を創建したのが当社の起源である。その後[[1766年]]（[[明和]]3年）に社殿を再建した際に、松崎家より道真像の掛軸が寄進された。隣の[[常泉院 (加須市)|常泉院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、890-891p</ref>。
[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[戦後]]長らく当社の信仰が薄れていたが、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）になり、氏子は神社護持を決議し、以降徐々に再建されていった<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[加須インターチェンジ|加須IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:加須市の歴史]] [[Category:加須市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

== 歴史 == [[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）に創建された。[[館林藩]]初代[[藩主]][[榊原康政]]の家臣に「松崎勘兵衛」という人物がおり、学問・人徳に優れていた。康政は勘兵衛を評価し、[[後陽成天皇]]筆の[[菅原道真]]像の[[掛軸]]を与えた。その後、道真と勘兵衛を慕う村人によって神社を創建したのが当社の起源である。その後[[1766年]]（[[明和]]3年）に社殿を再建した際に、松崎家より道真像の掛軸が寄進された。隣の[[常泉院 (加須市)|常泉院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、890-891p</ref>。
[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[戦後]]長らく当社の信仰が薄れていたが、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）になり、氏子は神社護持を決議し、以降徐々に再建されていった<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[加須インターチェンジ|加須IC]]より車15分。

== Tohoku Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tome)|ja|天神社 (登米市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}, Miyagi Prefecture.
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiroi)|ja|天神社 (白井市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Isumi) - Located in Isumi, Chiba Prefecture, in Misaki Town, Choja. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nishitokyo)|ja|天神社 (西東京市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo|ja|西東京市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mitaka)|ja|天神社 (三鷹市)}} - Located in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiki)|ja|天神社 (志木市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kamisato)|ja|天神社 (上里町五明)}} - Located in Kamisato, Saitama. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Chuo)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市中央区)}} - Located in Saitama City, Chuo Ward. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Minamiku-Futonoya)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市見沼区風渡野)}} - Located in Futonoya, Minamiku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Midoriku-Terayama)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市緑区寺山)}} - Located in Terayama, Midoriku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yashio-Ozone)|ja|天神社 (八潮市大曽根)}} - Located in Ozone, Yashio City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kuki-Kitahiroshima)|ja|天神社 (久喜市北広島)}} - Located in Kitahiroshima, Kuki City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Hanyu-Kamikawasaki)|ja|天神社 (羽生市上川崎)}} - Located in Kamikawasaki, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)|ja|天神社 (加須市平永)}} - Located in Hiranaga, Kazo City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yokosuka)|ja|天神社 (横須賀市)}} ({{ill|Kurihama Tenjinja|ja|久里浜天神社}}) - Located in {{ill|Yokosuka|ja|横須賀市}}, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kurobe)|ja|天神社 (黒部市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|ja|黒部市}}, Toyama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Fujikawaguchiko-Oarashi)|ja|天神社 (富士河口湖町大嵐)}} - Located in Oarashi, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|ja|富士河口湖町}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Oarashi Tenjinja|ja|大嵐天神社}} * {{ill|Tenmantenjinja (Iida)|ja|天満天神社 (飯田市)}} - Located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, Nagano Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Fumon-in (Iida)|ja|普門院 (飯田市)}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nagoya)|ja|天神社 (名古屋市中区)}} - Located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|ja|中区 (名古屋市)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Sakura Tenjinja|ja|桜天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Obu)|ja|天神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Nagasago, {{ill|Obu, Aichi|ja|大府市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Nagasago Tenjinja|ja|長草天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Toyokawa)|ja|天神社 (豊川市)}} - Located in Mikamicho, {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|ja|豊川市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Komaki)|ja|天神社 (小牧市)}} - Located in Shimazu, {{ill|Komaki|ja|小牧市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ Tenmantenjinja * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kasukawa)|ja|天神社 (春日井市勝川町)}} - Located in Katsukawacho, {{ill|Kasugai|ja|春日井市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kiyosu)|ja|天神社 (清須市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kiyosu|ja|清須市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mihama)|ja|天神社 (愛知県美浜町)}} - Located in {{ill|Mihama, Aichi|ja|美浜町 (愛知県)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kowa Tenjinja|ja|河和天神社}} * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|ja|北野天神社 (江南市)}} - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|ja|江南市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|ja|宝海天神社}} - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|ja|吉田天神社}} - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|ja|豊橋市}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|ja|天神社 (大阪市福島区)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|ja|福島区}}, Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|ja|淀川天神社}} * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|ja|桑津天神社}} * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|ja|豊中市}}, Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|ja|上新田天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|ja|天神社 (河内長野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kadoma)|ja|天神社 (門真市)}} - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|ja|門真市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|ja|天神社 (木津川市)}} - Located in Yamashiro, {{ill|Kizugawa|ja|木津川市}}, Kyoto Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nara-Takabatake)|ja|天神社 (奈良市高畑町)}} - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|ja|天神社 (大和高田市)}} - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|ja|天神社 (明石市)}} - Located in {{ill|Akashi|ja|明石市}}, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|ja|徳島眉山天神社}} - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|ja|眉山町}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|ja|天神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|ja|天神社 (徳島市入田町)}} - Located in Irita, Tokushima.
== Kyushu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Itoshima)|ja|天神社 (糸島市)}} - Located in Kamiaru, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture.

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kurobe)|ja|天神社 (黒部市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|ja|黒部市}}, Toyama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Fujikawaguchiko-Oarashi)|ja|天神社 (富士河口湖町大嵐)}} - Located in Oarashi, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|ja|富士河口湖町}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Oarashi Tenjinja|ja|大嵐天神社}} * {{ill|Tenmantenjinja (Iida)|ja|天満天神社 (飯田市)}} - Located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, Nagano Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Fumon-in (Iida)|ja|普門院 (飯田市)}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nagoya)|ja|天神社 (名古屋市中区)}} - Located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|ja|中区 (名古屋市)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Sakura Tenjinja|ja|桜天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Obu)|ja|天神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Nagasago, {{ill|Obu, Aichi|ja|大府市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Nagasago Tenjinja|ja|長草天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Toyokawa)|ja|天神社 (豊川市)}} - Located in Mikamicho, {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|ja|豊川市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Komaki)|ja|天神社 (小牧市)}} - Located in Shimazu, {{ill|Komaki|ja|小牧市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ Tenmantenjinja * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kasukawa)|ja|天神社 (春日井市勝川町)}} - Located in Katsukawacho, {{ill|Kasugai|ja|春日井市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kiyosu)|ja|天神社 (清須市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kiyosu|ja|清須市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mihama)|ja|天神社 (愛知県美浜町)}} - Located in {{ill|Mihama, Aichi|ja|美浜町 (愛知県)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kowa Tenjinja|ja|河和天神社}} * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|ja|北野天神社 (江南市)}} - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|ja|江南市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|ja|宝海天神社}} - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|ja|吉田天神社}} - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|ja|豊橋市}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|ja|天神社 (大阪市福島区)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|ja|福島区}}, Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|ja|淀川天神社}} * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|ja|桑津天神社}} * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|ja|豊中市}}, Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|ja|上新田天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|ja|天神社 (河内長野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kadoma)|ja|天神社 (門真市)}} - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|ja|門真市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|ja|天神社 (木津川市)}} - Located in Yamashiro, {{ill|Kizugawa|ja|木津川市}}, Kyoto Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nara-Takabatake)|ja|天神社 (奈良市高畑町)}} - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|ja|天神社 (大和高田市)}} - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|ja|天神社 (明石市)}} - Located in {{ill|Akashi|ja|明石市}}, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|ja|徳島眉山天神社}} - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|ja|眉山町}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|ja|天神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|ja|天神社 (徳島市入田町)}} - Located in Irita, Tokushima.
== Kyushu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Itoshima)|ja|天神社 (糸島市)}} - Located in Kamiaru, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture.
{{Aimai}}

(天神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | Location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

[[Category:Tenjinja]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Tenjinja]] [[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | Location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

[[Category:Tenjinja]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Tenjinja]] [[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | Location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | Location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

{{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | Location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

{{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

{{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

{{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

{{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>

[[en:Tenjinja]] [[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

pages]] [[en:Tenjinja]] [[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

pages]] [[en:Tenjinja]] [[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

(Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

[[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
