[[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | '''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

[[simple:Tenjinja]] [[ja:天神社]] [[Category:Tenjinja forks]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==

== Bibliography ==

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Tenjin shrines]] [[en:Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

[[simple:Tenjinja]]
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政|12=draft}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩|12=draft}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|12=draft}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|12=draft}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=draft}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|12=draft}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the expressway.

Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|12=draft}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=draft}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|12=draft}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the expressway.

[[simple:Tenjinja]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政|12=draft}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩|12=draft}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|12=draft}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|12=draft}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=draft}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|12=draft}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the expressway.

[[simple:Tenjinja]] {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|12=draft}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政|12=draft}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩|12=draft}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|12=draft}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|12=draft}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=draft}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|12=draft}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the expressway.

2 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:天神社 (加須市平永) up to rev 102264461
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=加須市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[加須市]][[平永]]1197 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 07 |緯度秒 = 44.2 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 33 |経度秒 = 30.0 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（[[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[加須市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）に創建された。[[館林藩]]初代[[藩主]][[榊原康政]]の家臣に「松崎勘兵衛」という人物がおり、学問・人徳に優れていた。康政は勘兵衛を評価し、[[後陽成天皇]]筆の[[菅原道真]]像の[[掛軸]]を与えた。その後、道真と勘兵衛を慕う村人によって神社を創建したのが当社の起源である。その後[[1766年]]（[[明和]]3年）に社殿を再建した際に、松崎家より道真像の掛軸が寄進された。隣の[[常泉院 (加須市)|常泉院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、890-891p</ref>。
[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[戦後]]長らく当社の信仰が薄れていたが、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）になり、氏子は神社護持を決議し、以降徐々に再建されていった<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[加須インターチェンジ|加須IC]]より車15分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} [[Category:加須市の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

== 歴史 == [[1615年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年）に創建された。[[館林藩]]初代[[藩主]][[榊原康政]]の家臣に「松崎勘兵衛」という人物がおり、学問・人徳に優れていた。康政は勘兵衛を評価し、[[後陽成天皇]]筆の[[菅原道真]]像の[[掛軸]]を与えた。その後、道真と勘兵衛を慕う村人によって神社を創建したのが当社の起源である。その後[[1766年]]（[[明和]]3年）に社殿を再建した際に、松崎家より道真像の掛軸が寄進された。隣の[[常泉院 (加須市)|常泉院]]が[[別当寺]]であった<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年、890-891p</ref>。
[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられた。[[戦後]]長らく当社の信仰が薄れていたが、[[1975年]]（[[昭和]]50年）になり、氏子は神社護持を決議し、以降徐々に再建されていった<ref name="神社"></ref>。

== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』埼玉県神社庁、1986年

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna era|qq=}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|ja|加須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Genna era|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna era|qq=}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|ja|榊原康政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakakibara Yasumasa|qq=}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|ja|館林藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebayashi Domain|qq=}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|ja|後陽成天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Yōzei|qq=}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa era|ja|明和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa era|qq=}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa era|qq=}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. {{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|07|44.2|N|139|33|30.0|E|display=title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.
