'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|qq=|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]], 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kazo, Saitama|Kazo, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna era]]. A retainer of [[:en:Sakakibara Yasumasa|Sakakibara Yasumasa]], the first lord of the [[:en:Tatebayashi Domain|Tatebayashi Domain]], named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by [[:en:Emperor Go-Yōzei|Emperor Go-Yōzei]]. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the [[:en:Meiwa|Meiwa era]]), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]]), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131900999]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986.

Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

== Bibliography ==

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama|1=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era|1=Yuanhe}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama|1=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era|1=Yuanhe}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa|1=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain|1=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei|1=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era|1=Meiwa}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (加須市平永)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Tenjinsha (天神社) | image = {{image requested|cat=Kazo City}} | location = {{ill|Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja|平永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranaga, Kazo, Saitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama|1=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, 1197 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128944|N|139.558333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Established = First year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era|1=Yuanhe}}, 1615 | Main Hall = | Other Names = | Pilgrimage Route = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Kazo|WD=Q851680|1=Kazo, Saitama}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kazo, Saitama|en|Kazo, Saitama|WD=Q851680|lt=Kazo, Saitama|1=Kazo, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History ==
The shrine was founded in 1615, during the first year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna era|1=Yuanhe}}. A retainer of {{ill|Sakakibara Yasumasa|en|Sakakibara Yasumasa|WD=Q1062738|lt=Sakakibara Yasumasa|1=Sakakibara Yasumasa}}, the first lord of the {{ill|Tatebayashi Domain|en|Tatebayashi Domain|WD=Q3034548|lt=Tatebayashi Domain|1=Tatebayashi Domain}}, named Matsuzaki Kanbee (松崎勘兵衛), was known for his scholarly excellence and virtue. Recognizing his abilities, Yasumasa presented him with a hanging scroll of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, inscribed by {{ill|Emperor Go-Yōzei|en|Emperor Go-Yōzei|WD=Q349440|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei|1=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}. Later, local villagers, inspired by their reverence for both Michizane and Kanbee, established the shrine.
In 1766 (the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|WD=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa era|1=Meiwa}}), the shrine was reconstructed, and at that time, the Matsuzaki family donated the Sugawara no Michizane scroll to the shrine. The neighboring {{ill|Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja|常泉院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōsen-in (Kazo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō-ji|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō-ji|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (guardian temple).<ref name="shrine">Saitama Shrine Office Research Group, *Shrines of Saitama: Iruma, North Saitama, and Chichibu* (『埼玉の神社 入間・北埼玉・秩父』), Saitama Shrine Office, 1986, pp. 890-891.</ref>
In 1876 (9th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), the shrine was officially designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. After World War II, the shrine's significance gradually declined, but in 1975 (50th year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}), the local parishioners resolved to preserve it, leading to gradual restoration efforts.<ref name="shrine"></ref>
== Access ==
* 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Kazo Interchange|ja|加須インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazo Interchange|1=Kazo interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10900948}} on the expressway.
